node.statement: (Main Dimension Bound). Let A be a unital *-algebra, a_1,...,a_m ∈ A^{sa}, β_1,...,β_m ∈ R, and C = {φ ∈ S(A) : φ(a_j) = β_j}. If φ is an extreme point of C, then dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) ≤ m + 1. Proof: By Node 1.1 (face-commutant correspondence), dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1. By Node 1.2 (Weis-Shirokov), since φ is extreme in C, dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ≤ m. Combining: dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1 ≤ m, hence dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) ≤ m + 1.
